Items.Title: 如何将 Chatgpt 集成到 VSCode IDE 中 | How to intergrate Chatgpt into VSCode IDE
Items.Description:
我一直在探索 ChatGPT 可以做什么，目前正在学习 Go 编程语言。 我认为在我学习 Go 时使用 ChatGPT 作为资源可能会有所帮助。 如果您对 Go 有任何疑问或需要语言特定方面的帮助，ChatGPT 可以提供解释和指导。 请注意，ChatGPT 无法执行代码或直接与您的代码编辑器交互，但它可以在您处理 Go 项目时提供有用的见解。

我最初使用 ChatGPT UI 与模型交互，但我发现在应用程序之间复制和粘贴文本很不方便。 那是我发现ChatGPT Helper VSCode 扩展的时候。 它使我能够：

  1. 询问为什么我的代码不起作用
  2. 请求解释我的代码
  3. 提出一个自由形式的问题

这个扩展让我在处理 Go 项目时更容易使用 ChatGPT。

设置

设置一切都很容易。 这是您需要的：

在你的机器上安装一个 ChatGPT 帐户 VSCode ChatGPT Helper

拥有所有这些项目后，您将需要创建一个 OpenAPI 密钥。 为此，请访问beta.openai.com/account/api-keys并单击“创建新密钥”。

复制密钥并打开 VSCode。 在 Windows 机器上，您可以通过按 Ctrl + Shift + P 键打开命令面板。 在 Mac 上，使用 Command + Shift + P 键。 这将弹出一个提示，您可以在其中粘贴密钥。

要使用 ChatGPT，您需要输入文本，例如“hello world”。 然后系统会要求您提供之前创建的 API 密钥。 将密钥粘贴到提示中，然后按 Enter。

接下来，您可以使用命令面板提出问题。 在 Windows 机器上，您可以通过按 Ctrl + Shift + P 键打开命令面板。 在 Mac 上，使用 Command + Shift + P 键。 这将弹出一个提示，您可以在其中输入问题。

例子

以下是 Go 语言中 ChatGPT Helper 功能的几个示例。

问为什么你的代码不起作用？

 package main func main() { fmt.Println("hello world") }

下面是输出

要求解释你的代码

package main import "fmt" func main() { fmt.Println("hello world") }

下面是输出

问一个自由形式的问题

下面是输出

<code>package main import ( "fmt" ) func main() { fmt.Println("Hello, playground") for i := 0; i < 100; i++ { fmt.Println("Hello, playground") } } </code> A: You can use <code>strings.Repeat</code>: https://play.golang.org/p/RQx6z0J8-q3. It's not the most efficient way to do it, but it's a simple way to do it. If you want to be more efficient, you can use <code>bytes.Buffer</code> and <code>io.CopyN</code>. See https://play.golang.org/p/hZj_5YtX-q3 for an example of that approach. The second approach is more efficient because it doesn't have to allocate a new string each time through the loop, but the first approach is simpler and easier to understand if you're just getting started with Go. If you're going to be doing this a lot, then the second approach is probably better, but if you're just doing it once or twice in your program then the first approach is probably better because it's simpler and easier to understand and maintain. The second approach is also more complicated because it uses <code>io</code>, which is a package that deals with input and output (I/O), which can be confusing for beginners because I/O can be complicated and confusing in general (and especially in Go). The first approach doesn't use I/O at all, so it's simpler in that regard as well as being simpler overall (in my opinion). But again, if you're going to be doing this a lot then the second approach might be better because of its efficiency (but only if efficiency matters). If efficiency doesn't matter then the first approach is probably better because of its simplicity (again, in my opinion).

结论

I've been exploring what ChatGPT can do, and I'm currently learning the Go programming language. I thought it might be helpful to use ChatGPT as a resource while I'm learning Go. ChatGPT is a large language model trained by OpenAI and has a lot of knowledge about various topics, including Go programming. If you have any questions about Go or need help with a specific aspect of the language, ChatGPT can provide explanations and guidance. Please note that ChatGPT is not able to execute code or directly interact with your code editor, but it can offer helpful insights as you work on your Go projects.

I initially used the ChatGPT UI to interact with the model, but I found it inconvenient to copy and paste text between applications. That's when I discovered the ChatGPT Helper VSCode extension. It gives me the ability to:

  1. Ask why my code isn't working
  2. Request explanations for my code
  3. Pose a free-form question

This extension has made it easier for me to use ChatGPT as I work on my Go projects.

Setup

A ChatGPT account VSCode installed on your machine ChatGPT Helper

Once you have all these items, you'll need to create an OpenAPI key. To do this, go to beta.openai.com/account/api-keys and click on "Create new secret key."

Copy the key and open VSCode. On a Windows machine, you can open the command palette by pressing the Ctrl + Shift + P keys. On a Mac, use the Command + Shift + P keys. This will bring up a prompt where you can paste the key.

To use ChatGPT, you'll need to enter a text, like "hello world." You'll then be asked for the API Secret key that you created earlier. Paste the key into the prompt and press Enter.

Next, you can use the command palette to ask a question. On a Windows machine, you can open the command palette by pressing the Ctrl + Shift + P keys. On a Mac, use the Command + Shift + P keys. This will bring up a prompt where you can enter your question.

Examples

Items.Title: ReactJS 的局限性是什么？ | What are the limitations of ReactJS?
Items.Description:
潘卡吉库马尔

发表于 12 月 27 日

这也是在 Reactjs 的新手级别或专家级别面试中被问到的一个非常常见的问题。

这意味着我们不能使用 ReactJS 开发一个完整的前端应用程序。 但是为了开发一个成熟的应用程序，我们需要安装其他第三方包，如 Redux、Mobx、React Router 等。如果我们谈论 Angular，它是一个框架，内置了所有这些功能，对第三方的依赖是Angular 非常少。 对第三方包的更多依赖会在将应用程序升级到最新版本时产生问题，因为这会产生兼容性问题，这意味着将应用程序升级到最新版本是一项非常繁琐的工作。

由于使用 Angular 这样的另一个框架，模板部分基本上是一个 HTML 代码，其中绑定了数据，因此对于 React 初学者来说，模板部分的工作非常容易。 使用 ReactJS 创建的默认应用程序不支持 SEO。 开发对 SEO 友好的应用程序需要大量的 ReactJS 专业知识，以便该应用程序能够被 Google 等搜索引擎正确索引。

Pankaj Kumar

Posted on Dec 27

ReactJS is one of the most popular javascript libraries and with so many advantages there are also few limitations that we should take care of while starting a project from scratch. It is also a very common question asked in the fresher level or expert level interview of Reactjs.

Limitations with ReactJS

1. It's a library, not a framework

ReactJS library Covers only the UI Layers(create reusable components) of the app and nothing else. Which means we can not develop a full fledge front application using ReactJS. But for developing a full-fledged application we need to install other third-party packages like Redux, Mobx, React Router, etc. If we talk about Angular which is a framework, has all these features inbuilt that the dependencies on the third party is very less with Angular. And more dependecy on third party packages will create issues while upgrading the application to the latest version as it will create problems of compatibility, means upgrading the app to latest is very tedious job.

2. Use of JSX

Since with another framework like Angular, the template part is basically an HTML code in which data is bound so working on the template part is very easy for a React beginners. With ReactJS JSX(mix of HTML and JavaScript) is used which is a bit different than HTML code which is a little bit of time taking to understand for learners.

3. Lack of proper documentation

React is updating and accelerating very fastly which is not maintained in the document at the same time, due to that it's very difficult for a new developer to understand it. The documentation of ReactJS is not good as Angular and also the content/Community Support of ReactJS is a little less than Angular.

4. Problem with SEO

The default apps created with ReactJS do not support SEO. It requires much expertise in ReactJS to develop an application that is SEO friendly so that the application will be properly indexed by Search Engines like Google.
